tol_time:
Which one of the sections within the last strobe sessions caused you to experience  discomfort. This can be indicated by the time between tones (section 1 the beginning of the session until the first bell tone, section 2 after the first bell tone etc.). Tick all that apply:

In some of the sessions you will have been shown frequency changes (at the first, second and 3rd tone), if you experienced discomfort during those changes, please select the 'Tone 1', 'Tone 2' or 'Tone 3' as appropriate.
tol_notes: Can you tell us anything else about when and how you experienced uncomfortable symptoms during the last session?
{"ImportId":"QID12_TEXT"}
at the first frequency it took my eyes a while to get used to the flashing and was a little bit uncomfortable
after the third frequency change, my eyes were very flickery and there were a lot of different coloured images in my head which made it a little uncomfortable
my eyes got quite sore behind them and it was hard to hold them closed
i could see a lot of different colours and images at once which made it a bit overwealming when trying to concentrate.
the slower flashing makes me feel like i have to blink a lot but it wasnt really uncomfortable, just a weird sensation
the slower flashing caused some images in my head that were spinning and with the addition of the slower flashing it made me a little bit dizzy and nausous
in the third section there was a point where lines were flickering up and down which made be a bit dizzy however it went away when the frequency changed to the next one.
it made head sting for a sec
During the more frequent strobing, it is harder for me to focus on any one 'object/shape' which I found distressing
The symptoms happened when the lights were flashing the most.
Mostly during the first half of the session, when the lights were brighter and flashing more consistently.
there was a circle spinning very fast which made my eyes twitch
too many things happening at once

Before the first bell, I could feel pain behind my eyes and I had the urge to squint my eyes so I could experience less of the light. As the section changed from 1 to 2 I felt a bit queasy and still had some pain in my eyes but after section 2 I did not experience any more uncomfortable side effects.
I experienced behind behind my eyes and queasiness throughout the session. I started to get a headache during section 4 along with the feeling of pressure in my head.

I experienced the symptoms during the first two sections and then after the third and fourth section I felt a lot more comfortable and most of the symptoms were gone. I still experienced a bit of a headache.

During the first two sections, I felt moderate pressure behind my eyes and a slight headache. This made me feel a bit anxious and uncomfortable but after the second session I could not feel the pressure anymore and I started to feel more relaxed and sleepy.
I had a headache throughout all of the sections. However, I could still enjoy the session as it was only mild. Most of the session was enjoyable.

In the beginning of the session, I could feel pressure behind my eyes and after a while that slowly went away. I had a headache throughout the session and I felt that along with pressure in my head. I could also feel my left eye twitching halfway through as if it wanted to close more. These symptoms made me feel more awake and alert.
in the second section, head started feeling heavy with slight pulsating. Vertigo effect was also stronger.
last section of light strobes were a bit more intense following the previous 3 sections so was a little disorientating for my eyes

Rather than uncomfortable, I felt a sense of unease. I did not like the colours (red), shapes (cheetah patterns) or movement (only one section was moving from side to side versus the rest of the 'picture.' These symptoms usually happened when the flashes were really fast and I had 'warmed' up (usually happened after the third tone)
Seemed maybe slower, worse
headache in 1/2, eyelids jumping in 2/3, 4 fine
tone 2 onwards eyelids jumping, tone 3 onwards headache
no
first tone made me the most anxious, with tone two maintaining this and tone three bringing it down slightly but tone four alleviated this the most
tense/pressure in my head sort of intensified
section one made me slightly uncomfortable but it wasn't until section four that I felt queasy
My mouth felt a bit dry
Headache in the back area
the large black and red geometry that i could 'see' brought slight negative emotions
My eyes felt more pressure after it had stopped
The first session the gaps were slower turning on and off which made it more blaring
Still pressure behind my eyes, but this session didnt feel as 'intense'
I saw less shapes and patterns than usual, this was just blaring red and black
The red and black in my eyes is quite uncomfortable but then in section 4 the colour changed to white and blue which was more pleasant
section 4 was really 'thick', its nicer when the patterns are small with small lines
I saw more pleasant colours than usual, with pink blue and yellow together which is pretty. The flashing light might be contributing to a slight headache though (i have a small cold)
Section 4 was a lot more blaring with bigger time gaps between light and no light
Section 3 was really cool especially at the beginning, and then section 4 was too much, it was overwhelming
If I 'looked' directly at the light it was worse than if my eyes were pointed more upwards
Section 2 felt kind of like when you are really drunk and the room is spinning
The switch between the slow flash in session 1 and the slightly faster flash in session 2 reminded me a lot of the way my heart rate accelerates when I have panic attacks
some of them made me feel a bit claustrophobic
Physically fine just felt a nauseous feeling similar to motion sickness
Eyes felt strained in a similar way to when you look directly at the sun
Moderate headache towards the end of the session.
